medications: Hydrocortisone 1% cream (short courses for dermatitis flares)
Naproxen 250 mg as needed for pain/inflammation (limited use)
Ferrous sulfate 325 mg every other day (iron)
health.mental_health: Generally resilient but under sustained stress from balancing school counseling workload with competitive training; occasional sleep disruption and performance anxiety before competitions. Keeps this private at work to avoid being seen as less reliable.
recent_visits: Sports medicine follow-up for ACL rehab progression (last month, Colonia Roma clinic)
Physical therapy session for knee strength and return-to-sport testing (weekly)
Dermatology appointment for acne/dermatitis management (2 months ago, Hospital Ángeles consultorio)
Orthopedics consult for shoulder pain after a training block (6 weeks ago)

finance.income_range: MXN $22,000–$30,000 per month (net, school counselor salary with occasional workshop stipends)
finance.debt: Owes ~MXN $85,000 on a personal loan used for ACL surgery/rehab costs not fully covered; also carries a credit card balance that fluctuates around MXN $8,000–$12,000 during competition seasons.
investments: Small CETES Directo holdings (short-term government bonds)
recent_transactions: MXN $1,450 physical therapy package payment (Polanco clinic)
MXN $980 dermatology pharmacy purchase (topicals and shampoo)
MXN $1,200 athletic tape, knee sleeve, and resistance bands

relationships: Mariana López (training partner and close friend from the track club)
Aarav Singh (older cousin in Guadalajara; confidant about family expectations and identity)
Sofía Reyes (on-and-off romantic interest; supportive but frustrated by Quinn’s schedule)
Coach Iván Ortega (primary coach; high expectations and strict training plans)
Small circle of fellow counselors at school who sometimes cover shifts during competitions
social.social_media_activity: Posts training clips and race photos on Instagram a few times a week, keeps personal life limited; uses a private Close Friends list for injury updates and mental lows. Avoids posting anything that could identify students or school incidents.
private_conversations: Discussing non-binary identity and pronoun use with family members who are not fully supportive
Talking with coach about pain management and pressure to compete despite shoulder symptoms
Confiding in a friend about a student crisis that affected them emotionally (without identifying details)
Negotiating boundaries with Sofía about time, intimacy, and travel during competition season

schedule.work_hours: Monday–Friday, 7:30 a.m.–3:30 p.m. at a private secondary school; occasional evening parent meetings once or twice a month.
regular_appointments: Physical therapy: Tuesdays and Thursdays, 6:30–7:30 p.m.
Strength & conditioning: Mondays, Wednesdays, Fridays, 6:00–7:15 a.m.
Track training: Tuesdays and Thursdays, 5:15–6:15 a.m.; long session Saturday morning
Dermatitis/acne routine: nightly skincare; ketoconazole shampoo on set days
Monthly check-in with sports medicine physician (during rehab phases)
upcoming_events: Inter-school well-being workshop facilitation (next Wednesday, after school)
Regional athletics meet in Toluca (two weeks from Saturday; travel planned)
Parent-teacher counseling block for graduating students (end of month)
Follow-up shoulder assessment if pain persists (tentatively booked for next month)
schedule.daily_routine: Early training before work, counseling sessions and crisis support during the school day, short nap or meal prep after work, PT or mobility work in the evening, then planning notes for student follow-ups. Protects recovery time tightly to avoid flare-ups.

professional.company: Private secondary school in Benito Juárez (Colegio Nuevo Horizonte)
projects: Implementing a peer-mentoring program for first-year students
Coordinating referrals with external psychologists for high-risk cases
Delivering workshops on exam anxiety and study skills for graduating classes
Updating the school’s crisis response protocol and documentation workflow
professional.performance_issues: Occasional fatigue during afternoon sessions after double-training days; has requested a couple of schedule adjustments for competition travel. Worries that taking time off for rehab could be interpreted as lack of commitment, so they sometimes overextend.
workplace_relationships: Close collaboration with the school psychologist (Dra. Paola Méndez) on referrals
Tension with an assistant principal who prioritizes attendance metrics over counseling time
Trusted rapport with a few homeroom teachers who flag student issues early

hobbies: Weekend café reading (sports psychology and fiction)
Exploring neighborhoods by bike (when knee allows)
habits: Tracks training load, sleep, and pain levels in a private app to prevent re-injury
Uses ice/compression on knee after intense sessions
Limits alcohol during training blocks; occasional drinks after meets
High caffeine intake on early mornings (coffee plus occasional pre-workout)
Strict skincare routine to manage acne/dermatitis; avoids fragranced products
lifestyle.living_situation: Shares a two-bedroom apartment in Narvarte with a roommate to keep costs manageable; keeps a small home setup for rehab (bands, foam roller, stationary bike) and prioritizes quiet evenings for recovery.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: High-protein, performance-focused diet with lots of rice, beans, eggs, chicken, and vegetables; moderates dairy when acne flares; stays hydrated and uses electrolytes during hot training days.
